text:
User: you are giving far too much weight to this PRODUCT_MODEL.md -- that's the wrong approach -- the right approach is for inject and capture to query / persist an ever-evolving guide of everything learned about the project and about the user in general; a single PRODUCT_MODEL.md is COMPLETELY the wrong approach -- we want to anchor and grow knowledge exactly as detailed in https://github.com/nvk/llm-wiki (delegate a sonnet agent to review that) so that we can end up with incredibly reach and detailed guidelines that capture ALL the nunance and ALL the known details of something extremely specific -- imagine if the user was working on the sched() function of the linux kernel, that's very detailed info that wouldn't simply be dumped in a generic PRODUCT_MODEL.md, that's something that would go into a very detailed, very specifici wiki about how the scheduler works in great level of detail -- that level of detail is captured by the `capture` part and if the user says "hey, how does sched() work in a RT kernel" that's something that the agent in the `inject` side will see a list of all compiled guides, notice one is titled "Guide on sched() and process scheduling in the linux kernel" and immediately (probably) jump off to read that one and any other wiki that links to something related to be able to inject into claude code something immediately relevant.

THAT is the goal of the project.

And in a way, this prompt I'm giving you here is a good example -- I gave you something super rich here, and it will just be dumped in a conversation history, but if the `capture` hook were working properly, this would enrich a bunch of detail and nuance about the product, about how I think, about what I value, etc, etc.

Assistant: This is a real reframing, and I've got it — let me play it back to make sure, because it changes the spine of the project:

**The wrong model (current):** `capture` synthesizes everything into one generic `PRODUCT_MODEL.md`; `inject` prefetches that single blob. That flattens nuance — everything competes for space in one file and gets diluted.

**The right model (your goal):** an ever-evolving **wiki** of many interlinked, topic-specific guides — exactly the llm-wiki model. `capture` *grows* the wiki: rich, deep, specific entries (a "How `sched()` and process scheduling work in the Linux kernel" guide holds genuine depth, cross-linked to related entries), not a paragraph in a catch-all. `inject` *navigates* the wiki: it sees the **index of guide titles**, recognizes the relevant one ("Guide on sched()…"), reads it, **follows its links** to related guides, and injects exactly the dense, relevant detail. Knowledge is *anchored and grown*, not dumped and diluted. [...] - **Global wiki not implemented** — global scope lessons go to `pending-lessons.md` queue unchanged per spec. Only project-scoped guides are wiki-compiled. [...] - Global-scope wiki not implemented (globals still queue to `pending-lessons.md` per spec).
